author: mark
text:
Config: clamp id_length, reject escaping worktree_dir

Config::load now clamps id_length into [4, 32] (warning on clamp) and
rejects worktree_dir values starting with '/' or containing '..'. The
prior loader trusted whatever was in .balls/config.json, so id_length=0
infinite-looped id generation and a worktree_dir of '../evil' or
'/tmp/evil' would have escaped the repo root.

Generation retry split out of basic.rs into a new id_gen module so the
collision and exhaustion paths can be unit-tested deterministically with
a closure-based oracle. Previously coverage of those paths leaned on an
integration test that forced id_length=1 — now forbidden by the clamp.
That test is replaced with one asserting bl create still works after the
loader clamps an out-of-range id_length.

100% coverage, clippy clean, line limits clean.
